reason: Modifying /etc/fstab to add a mount option changes the filesystem mounting process. An error can prevent /home from mounting, leading to login failures, loss of user data access, and potentially lock out SSH or console access. According to policy, filesystem/partition changes are classified as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; Potential boot or mount failure; Loss of access to user home directories; Possible SSH login disruption; System may become unbootable if fstab syntax is incorrect
